topic: Validate and stress-test the merge design for folding deep-loop-runtime into deep-loop-workflows as system-deep-loop: structural layout, bidirectional path-coupling repair, the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow, reference migration across commands/agents/READMEs/advisor-corpus, and whether fallback-router.ts should be wired for real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback.

noveltyJustification: First evidence pass; most findings are new to this lineage and answer the structural identity question.
keyQuestions: Is the proposed system-deep-loop/runtime/ structural layout correct, or does it create mode/skill identity ambiguity?
answeredQuestions: Is the proposed system-deep-loop/runtime/ structural layout correct, or does it create mode/skill identity ambiguity?

ruledOut.reason: The registry models public workflow modes only; runtime is backendKind infrastructure and packet-level graph metadata is prohibited.

keyFindings.title: Runtime should be nested infrastructure, not a workflow mode
keyFindings.summary: The hub/runtime split already claims a single workflow identity over a consumed backend; merging runtime under system-deep-loop/runtime preserves that architecture.

keyFindings.title: Fresh-authored unified graph metadata is safer than merging two graph files
keyFindings.summary: Runtime graph metadata currently duplicates edges back to workflows; fresh authoring removes now-internal edges.

noveltyJustification: The main rules were expected, but this pass found a concrete missing internal executable path and a broader runtime-test inventory.
keyQuestions: Are the Class A and Class B path-repair rules complete and directionally correct for live source files and tests?
answeredQuestions: Are the Class A and Class B path-repair rules complete and directionally correct for live source files and tests?

ruledOut.approach: Blind repo-wide replacement for path coupling
ruledOut.reason: Grep hits mix executable imports, fixture expectations, and historical/prose text; each category needs separate handling.

keyFindings.title: Class A and Class B path-direction rules are correct
keyFindings.summary: Runtime-to-workflows keeps hop count and deletes the sibling segment; workflows-to-runtime loses one hop and targets runtime/.

keyFindings.title: replay-graph-from-artifacts is an unlisted internal repair site
keyFindings.summary: The council graph replay script hardcodes the runtime upsert path and should be repaired in phase 002 with internal executable paths.

keyFindings.title: Runtime unit-test inventory needs explicit classification
keyFindings.summary: Ten unit-test files mention deep-loop-workflows; the plan should classify code-path fixtures separately from historical text.

focus: System-spec-kit tooling borrow and hidden seams
noveltyJustification: This pass confirms the four listed borrow edits but adds two phase-002-blocking omissions: artifact-root and a test:council-included path-integrity test.
keyQuestions: Is the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow repair correctly scoped to phase 002, and are there hidden borrow paths beyond the four planned files?
answeredQuestions: Is the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow repair correctly scoped to phase 002, and are there hidden borrow paths beyond the four planned files?

ruledOut.approach: Decouple runtime from system-spec-kit compiler/typeRoots during the merge
ruledOut.reason: The merge can repair path depth only; decoupling compiler dependencies is separate hardening and would conflate failure modes.

keyFindings.title: artifact-root is a hidden system-spec-kit seam
keyFindings.summary: artifact-root.cjs re-exports system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs through a depth-sensitive path and must be repaired in phase 002.

keyFindings.title: test:council needs council-playbook-anchor-integrity path updates
keyFindings.summary: The test:council script includes council-playbook-anchor-integrity, which hardcodes deep-loop-runtime test paths and regexes.

noveltyJustification: The broad category plan was expected, but this pass corrected stale assumptions, confirmed current advisor/scorer paths, and found a duplicate graph-edge cleanup detail.
keyQuestions: Is the external reference migration plan complete across commands, agents, READMEs, hooks, advisor corpus, skill graph, generated contracts, and examples?
answeredQuestions: Is the external reference migration plan complete across commands, agents, READMEs, hooks, advisor corpus, skill graph, generated contracts, and examples?

ruledOut.reason: The compiled contract header identifies compile-command-contracts.cjs as the generator; source assets and router files must be edited then regenerated.

keyFindings.title: External migration plan is directionally complete and category-driven
keyFindings.summary: The 003 plan covers hardcoded constants, structured identity fields, codegen, command assets, docs, graph metadata, examples, advisor corpus, and final verification as separate categories.

keyFindings.title: Advisor projection migration must be synchronized
keyFindings.summary: Python, TypeScript scorer files, and the drift guard all hardcode the merged deep skill identity and registry/projection contract.

keyFindings.title: Hook, CI, plugin, and doctor surfaces are live migration targets
keyFindings.summary: The mirror checker, GitHub workflow, loop guard, and parent-skill doctor checker all contain old deep-loop paths or assumptions.

keyFindings.title: Sibling graph migration should dedupe duplicate cli-opencode edges
keyFindings.summary: The migration should preserve meaningful contextual edges while collapsing literal duplicate targets.

noveltyJustification: The router's existence was expected, but this pass found a plan-level design correction: model substitution context lives in fanout-run.cjs or a pool hook, not naturally inside the generic pool primitive.
keyQuestions: Should fallback-router.ts be wired now for GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback, or remain optional/deferred?
answeredQuestions: Should fallback-router.ts be wired now for GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback, or remain optional/deferred?

ruledOut.approach: Treat fallback-router wiring as required for merge closeout
ruledOut.reason: The phase is P2 optional and needs config/schema/integration-test work unrelated to rename/path/reference migration.

ruledOut.approach: Wire model substitution directly into the generic pool without a hook
ruledOut.reason: fanout-pool.cjs only owns generic items/retries; model-aware command construction and lineage context live in fanout-run.cjs.

keyFindings.title: fallback-router is shipped and validates fallback graphs
keyFindings.summary: The router validates graph safety and resolves failed models to fallback or fail-fast decisions.

keyFindings.title: router unit tests do not prove fanout GLM/MiMo integration
keyFindings.summary: Pure routing behavior is tested, but there is no forced-failure fanout test for GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro.

keyFindings.title: fanout-pool only requeues same-label retries
keyFindings.summary: The retry branch increments retryCounts and pushes the same item index back to the queue.

keyFindings.title: model-aware fallback belongs in fanout-run or an explicit pool hook
keyFindings.summary: fanout-run owns lineage model/kind, prompt, command construction, and failure context.

keyFindings.title: GLM to MiMo fallback is optional feature work, not merge-critical
keyFindings.summary: The feature needs a registry/config surface and integration test, and the phase is explicitly P2 optional.

noveltyJustification: This pass added only convergence-state confirmation; it introduced no new substantive design findings.

ruledOut.approach: Continue to max-10 after all questions are answered
ruledOut.reason: The configured stop policy is convergence, the minimum iteration count is satisfied, and no blocked stop remains.

keyFindings.title: Legal convergence reached after all key questions answered
keyFindings.summary: All five key questions are answered, no blocked stop remains, and the minimum iteration count is satisfied.
